observations.recommendation:
We recommended –
The OIC-Municipal Accountant:
closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve the missing disbursement vouchers and lacking documents, and to draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs).
reclassify the two (2) bank accounts from the current account to Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Savings Account, to maintain the faithful presentation of the Cash account in the financial statements.
The Municipal Treasurer:
closely coordinate with the OIC – Municipal Accountant and take immediate action for any dormant bank account under custody.
During the exit conference, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer assured the audit team that the recommendations would be implemented.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant draws a journal entry to record the motor vehicle received from the DOH;
The Supply Officer issues the PAR of the motor vehicles to the end-user; and
Henceforth, the Management provides the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant copies of transfer and receipt invoices, such as Property Transfer Report and Deed of Donations for subsequent donations that the Municipality may receive.
The LGU assured the audit team that they will coordinate with the Donors to secure the Deed of Donation and Property Transfer Report. Once they secure the supporting documents, the OIC-Municipal Accountant will draw the JEV to record the motor vehicles, and the Supply Officer will issue the PAR.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw journal entries to derecognize in the books of account the PPEs costing below ₱50,000.00, that were issued to end-users prior to CY 2024.
We also recommended that the Supply Officer ensure the issuance of the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slips for monitoring and proper accountability of these semi-expendable property.
The Accountant assured the audit team that she would draw the journal entries to derecognize the recorded PPEs costing below ₱50,000.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive spearhead the compliance and strict adherence to the COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to their existence, condition, and accountability.
The LGU assured the audit team that they will conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-
effect the adjustments corresponding to the noted accounting errors based on relevant and sufficient documents; and
recognize an Accounts Payable only for obligations on completed infrastructure projects or for goods/services which have been accepted/rendered, and the claims are with proper and complete documents.
The accountant said that she will make the necessary adjustments and the LGU further assured the audit team that the recommendations will be implemented.

We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant strictly complies with the pertinent provisions of Section 127 of IPSAS 1, Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016, regarding the disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements, particularly for Cash in Bank – LCCA, PPE, and Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities.
The Accountant committed to implement the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant adhere to the highest degree of objectivity and consistency in keeping the accounts to ascertain correspondence in the balances and amounts reflected and presented in the financial statements and the corresponding notes.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant assured the audit team that she would comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that –
the LCE to instruct the officials and personnel concerned to open a Trust Fund bank account; and
the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended CY 2024 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund.
The LCE assured the audit team that they will open a Trust Fund bank account this 2025, and the unexpended CY 2024 LDRRMF will be transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommend that the OIC – Municipal Budget Officer, the MDRRMO, and the OIC - Municipal Accountant reconcile the LDRRMF balances presented in their respective reports.
Management assured the audit team that they will comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the OIC - MDRRMO submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
The LGU assured the audit team that they will comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly consider the required conditions before augmentation of funds to other items of the same expense class are approved to ascertain propriety and validity of the changes made to the budget
The LGU assured the audit team that the recommendation will be implemented.

We recommended that the Municipality execute an Individual Job Order Contract when they engage the services of a Job Order Personnel.
Management committed to implement the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all accountable officers and employees concerned to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise, withhold the payment of any money due them in accordance with Section 37 of PD No. 1445.
Management assured the audit team that they will send demand letters to all accountable officers and employees concerned and they will instruct them to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the LGU immediately renew the registration of its motor vehicles to prevent the accumulation of penalties and fines that may result therefrom.
Management committed to immediately renew the registration of their motor vehicles.

We recommended that the GFPS of the Municipality submits the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed by regulation.
The GFPS committed to submit the GPB and GAD AR within the prescribed deadline.

We recommended that the TWG of the Municipality’s GFPS to prepare the GAD Agenda to ensure proper monitoring and evaluation of GAD results and outcomes.
The Municipality’s GFPS assured the audit team that they will prepare the GAD Agenda.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant trace the exact amount of fund transfers erroneously credited to the Due to Philhealth account, and make the necessary adjustments.
The OIC-Accountant assured the audit team that she will trace the exact amount of fund transfers credited to the Due to Philhealth and make the necessary adjustments.
6. We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to achieve appropriate and correct balance of the Due to NGAs account in the financial statements.
9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately initiate the return of the undisbursed LGSF to the National Treasury to avoid inadvertent use of the fund other than its intended purpose.

We strongly recommended to the Local Chief Executive:

10. Formally demand from the Supplier on the complete delivery of procured items in accordance with the agreed specifications, and

11. Approve payment only for fully delivered items in good condition to safeguard the government funds from loss or wastage.

We recommended that the LCE strictly ensure that:

14. Traveling expenses of personnel attending the disaster-related training and seminars be charged against the regular appropriation of the MDRRM Office; and

15. Funds from LDRRMF are utilized for expenses relevant to disaster preparedness that will capacitate the community in efficiently managing all types of emergencies.

19. OIC-Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of each year, a duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (PRTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card; and

20. OIC-Municipal Accountant set-up the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables based on the list submitted by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, in compliance with the modified accrual method of accounting for income from real property taxes, in accordance with the NGAS Manual Volume I for LGUs.

25. We strongly recommended to the Local Chief Executive to stop the practice of using the Trust Fund other than its specific purpose and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 305 (e) of the Local Government Code and Section 4 (3) of PD No. 1445 to properly attain the objectives for which the trust fund was established.

30. OIC-Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant utilize the LDRRM funds for the specific purpose it was created and refrain from incurring and charging expenses that do not contribute to the advancement of LDRRM objectives
39. immediately cause the return to the TF the borrowed amount of ₱400,000.00 taken from the LGSF, and henceforth, only utilize the transferred funds for their intended specific purposes.

a. Instruct the OIC-Accountant to –

40. Recognize in the books all existing inventories of DRRM, and henceforth, coordinate with the designated Supply/Property Officer for the list of undistributed supplies, materials or relief goods at year end; and

41. Include in the Notes to Financial Statements the disclosure requirements on DRRM prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

b. Require the designated Supply/Property Officer for DRRM to –

42. Comply with the preparation and submission of the Inventory List of Items for DRRM, duly supported with Annexes P-1 and P-2, as required under COA Circular No. 2014-002; and

43. Conduct periodic reconciliation of property records with the accounting records and furnish the Accounting Office of pertinent reports relating to procured DRRM items, their distribution and balances at year end, in order that the OIC-Accountant could record the same in the books.

45. We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation, to be updated regularly, to serve as guide in the computation of periodic provision of depreciation.

46. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make adequate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

47. We recommended and that Local Chief Executive strictly observe the timetable set in the LGU’s Annual Procurement Plan to ensure timely disbursements and direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise more diligence in the monitoring of disbursements to ensure the availability of funds and prevent incurring bank service charges due to bounced checks.

observations.reason: The accountant attached a schedule of unliquidated cash advances of employees, listing down the unliquidated balances for each employee which has a balance of P2,689,322.27 as of December 31, 2020. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to refrain from charging administrative and non-development related expenses against the 20% Development Fund and strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.  

Moreover, charge the wages of the JO’s/Casuals assigned to income generating enterprise like the Minasangay Island Beach Resort to income from its operations.
observations.reason: Management maintained its contention that repairs and rehabilitation of infrastructure can be charged to DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU refrain from this irregular practice of borrowing funds from the Trust Fund to finance operating expenditures of the General Fund.  Moreover, stop charging Trust Fund for expenses which are not in accordance with the purpose for which the fund was received.
observations.reason: The LGU resorted to borrowing from the Trust Fund because of instances of immediate need of funds for the operations of the municipality and the non-availability/insufficiency of fund at that particular time in the general fund.  Though they intend to return the borrowed funds once they became available.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Inventory Committee, if any to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all properties of LGU.
Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.
observations.reason: The LGU still has no General Services Officer who can focus on the implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Management already started inventory of current road networks but failed to prepare and submit the report because there was no focal person assigned to accomplish the task.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, direct:

The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for each of the QRF, MOOE & CO.
The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to regularly prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund and submit to the Audit Team;
Further, the Municipal Accountant should determine the unexpended balances for QRF & MOOE and transfer the same to a special trust fund to be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality within the next five years.
observations.reason:
The Budget Officer did not prepare the RAO separately for QRF, MOOE & CO.

Coordination with the MDRRMO and the Municipal accountant was not in place to facilitate on-time submission of the report to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements for all funds maintained for Cash in Bank in pursuance to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 to 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to present a reliable and accurate balance of the account.
observations.reason: While the accountant was able to submit a bank reconciliation statement for the year end, preparation of the bank reconciliation statements should be made on a monthly basis to ease year-end report preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts and ensure correct computation based on the guidelines set in PPSAS 17. Then, prepare all the necessary journal entries to correct the account balances.
observations.reason: The accountant was able to apply depreciation for some PPEs for CY 2019, however, records were not available to them for other PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to submit the accounts, financial statements and reports and accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Quarterly reports are submitted but timeliness is not observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management form its Solid Waste Management Board and through it, comply strictly all the provisions of the Republic Act No. 9003 or otherwise known as the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law and to ensure protection of public health and environment and lessen against air pollution.

Moreover, prepare and submit Local Solid Waste Management Plans (10-year) to DENR-EMB for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.
observations.reason: The LGU is still making revisions for their 10-year EcoSWAM Plan to be submitted to EMB for review. They were also on the process of procuring a lot to be used as a sanitary landfill.

The concerned local officials to refrain charging Trust Fund transactions against the General Fund and immediately replenish the General Fund.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to make appropriate journal entries to correct the erroneous entry made.
observations.reason: The LGU still practice using Trust Fund for General Fund disbursements when the General Fund is depleted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive submit immediately all the stated required documents for the granted cash advances for Confidential Fund to the COA Audit Team for verification.

observations.reason: The LGU has already committed to comply with the submission of the documents but is unable to recover said files.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the financial reports and related documents to the Audit Team for audit.  

Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor should ensure that all officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided under Section 444.b.1.x of RA 7160, and  should see to it that laws, rules and regulations shall be strictly complied/followed.
observations.reason: Coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant on the submission of DVs was not properly in place to facilitate the timely submission to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall establish a GAD Focal Point System in order to efficiently come up with a GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the provisions in the afore-cited Joint Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete supply ledger cards and strictly adhere to the regulations on the recording of receipt and issuance of inventory items, as well as controlling and monitoring of the same. Also, to prepare adjusting entries for the noted deficiencies; and
Direct the Property Custodian to maintain complete stock cards, regularly prepare the RIS and RSMI, conduct the annual physical count of all inventories on stock as well as those transferred to distributing site to determine any balances on hand, and to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly abide with the prevailing regulations in computing for depreciation; do a recomputation and effect the appropriate adjustments to each PPE account;
Compute the correct depreciation for the completed infrastructure projects and adjust accordingly; and
Prepare Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation, to be updated regularly, to serve as guide in the computation of periodic provision of depreciation.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE will direct the accountant to make the necessary adjustments to the affected accounts according to Paragraph 44 and 45 of IPSAS 23 and to make appropriate disclosure in the Notes to FS regarding the balance of the BGCM Fund to provide a more accurate and reliable financial statement.
We recommended and management agreed that they will comply with the provision in Section 3.7 of LBC 125 for the creation of SAGF for the BGCM.
We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of LBC 125 on the allowable PPAs and expenses for the benefit of its constituents especially on the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended and management agreed that they will immediately submit the lacking documents and ensure that the required documents for emergency procurements (negotiated) are complete before payments are made in accordance with the provisions of the Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines set by GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 to prevent the possibility of abuses and corruption in the undertaking of its emergency procurements.

We recommended and management agreed to immediately comply on the posting and reporting requirements provided in Section 4 of LBC 125 for the utilization of its BGCM to provide adequate information and disclosure to its constituents.
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the liquidating officer to immediately apply for bond so as not to expose government funds to the risk of loss or misuse.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a)	ensure that henceforth, all PPAs that are to be implemented by the Municipality should be clearly specified as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken, and avoid lump sum appropriation; and
b)	instruct the MDC to prepare a Supplemental or Amended AIP for newly identified PPAs and cause the approval by the Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make adequate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the BAC submit APP-CSE either printed copy or electronic copy through online to PS-DBM for the succeeding years in order to include the needs of the Municipality of the commonly-use supplies in the projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities, and overall management in order to subsequently purchase all CSE in the PS-DBM depot.

We recommended and that Local Chief Executive strictly observe the timetable set in the LGU’s Annual Procurement Plan to ensure timely disbursements and direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise more diligence in the monitoring of disbursements to ensure the availability of funds and prevent incurring bank service charges due to bounced checks.

We recommended that:
The Treasurer should send a written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.
For improperly reported stale checks, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of Municipal Accountant, provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding; that they have been voided and no obligations exist.
The Accountant should make the necessary adjusting entries for stale-dated checks.
The Treasurer and Accountant should coordinate in the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the City Accountant to reconcile her Loans Payable record with that of the lending institutions so as to present fairly the financial statements.  Moreover, to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct: 
a) the OIC – Treasurer to be more diligent and consistent in his records to comply with the high standards set about by Section 111(2) of PD 1445. 
b) the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to submit all pertaining receipts, deposit slips and reports; and assist the OIC - Accountant to determine the cause/s of discrepancies and adjust the books accordingly in order to correct the reported balance of the Cash, Local Treasury account for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive designate permanent employees to act as collecting officers and require their immediate application for bond so as not to expose government funds to the risk of loss or misuse.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
